Records.description: One-loop massless triangle with three off-shell legs. Leading order is the Bloch-Wigner dilogarithm.

Records.authors: Abreu, Samuel, Britto, Ruth, Duhr, Claude, Gardi, Einan
Records.description: One-loop triangle with massless propagators and three nonzero external masses, finite in $D=4$. Weight-2 result in $z,\bar{z}$ variables. Single and double cuts computed, integral reconstructed from cuts via dispersion.
One-loop massless triangle in $d=2$ with generic propagator powers. Result: sv Gauss $_2F_1$.

Records.authors: Henn, Johannes, Pratt, Elizabeth, Sattelberger, Anna-Laura, Zoia, Simone
Records.description:
One-loop massless triangle integral with three off-shell external legs ($x_1=p_1^2, x_2=p_2^2, x_3=(p_1+p_2)^2$) in $d=4$, with general conformal weights specialized to $c_0=4, c_1=c_2=c_3=2$ (unit propagator powers $
u_1=
u_2=
u_3=1$). The integral satisfies a system of three conformal second-order PDEs generating a holonomic $D_3$-ideal $I_3$ of rank 4. The four linearly independent solutions (eq. 2.11) span the analytic continuation space and are expressed in terms of dilogarithms $\operatorname{Li}_2$ and logarithms. Canonical series solutions are computed via the SST (Saito-Sturmfels-Takayama) algorithm and independently recovered via Wasow's method for Fuchsian systems. Singular locus: $\{x_1=0\} \cup \{x_2=0\} \cup \{x_3=0\} \cup \{\lambda=0\}$ where $\lambda = x_1^2+x_2^2+x_3^2-2(x_1 x_2+x_1 x_3+x_2 x_3)$.
Records.location: Eq. (2.1), Fig. 1; analytic result Eq. (2.7); solutions Eqs. (2.11)-(2.14); conformal PDE system Eqs. (2.3)-(2.5)

Records.description: Multi-leg fishnet family, fishnet-multileg-2d_L1_massless-internal-2d. Multi-leg two-dimensional fishnet on the trivalent (hexagonal) tiling (arXiv:2402.19034 Eq. (2.1), Figs. 5-6): the triangle-track sequence $Z_L$. Position space: a chain of $L$ trivalent integration vertices; momentum space (planar dual, notes/families_sweep_batch/derivations.md): the $L$-triangle strip on vertices $w_1..w_{L+2}$ with path edges $\{w_i,w_{i+1}\}$ and chords $\{w_i,w_{i+2}\}$, one off-shell leg at every vertex; legs $= L+2$, loops $= L$ (Euler-exact on the dual). $Z_2$ reduces to the box and $Z_3$ to a one-loop pentagon by star-triangle (VALUE identities); the lowest geometry is a genus-2 Picard curve (Eq. (3.30)). Propagator weight $2/3$ per line is a value-level datum.

Generalised ladder family, generalised-ladder_L1_massless-internal-offshell-external. Drummond's generalised ladders (arXiv:1207.3824 Eq. (2.7)): three-point ladders indexed by loop order $L$ and a binary word $m \in \{0,1\}^{L-1}$; $2^{L-1}$ members per $L$. Seeded representative: the all-zeros word. Momentum-space graph (planar dual, notes/families_sweep_batch/derivations.md): the fan $F_L =$ hub $h$ joined to every vertex of a path $q_0..q_L$, legs at $q_0$, $q_L$, $h$; loops $= L$ (Euler-exact on the dual). The fan is NOT bare-isomorphic to the 4-point box ladder ($V = L+2$ vs $2L+2$); the relation to the box ladders is the value-level conformal completion. The 2-point vacuum limits of the word family are the zigzags and wheels.

Triangle ladder family, triangle-ladder_L1_massless-internal-offshell-external. The Usyukina-Davydychev triangle ladder $T^{(L)}$ consists of $(L{-}1)$ box cells capped by a triangle at the apex, with two rails of $L$ edges converging to a shared apex vertex and $L$ rungs connecting the rails at each level. It has $2L{+}1$ vertices, $3L$ massless internal propagators, $L$ loops, and three off-shell external legs at the two base vertices and the apex. The triangle ladder evaluates to the same $\Phi^{(L)}$ as the four-point box ladder and the generalised-ladder fan via the conformal magic identity, but is a genuinely different topology from both for $L \ge 2$: the triangle ladder has $V = 2L{+}1$, $E = 3L$ versus $V = L{+}2$, $E = 2L{+}1$ for the fan. Only $L = 1$ (the triangle) is shared. Value = PhiLadder[1, 1/3, 1/5].

Results.stCommand: STIntegrate[{{{{1, 2}, 0}, {{1, 3}, 0}, {{2, 3}, 0}}, {{1, Subscript[M, 1]}, {2, Subscript[M, 2]}, {3, Subscript[M, 3]}}}, Dimension -> "4 - 2*eps", Order -> 0, Substitutions -> {MM1 -> (1 - z)*(1 - zb), MM2 -> z*zb, MM3 -> 1}]
Results.resultTeXPreview: \left(\frac{4 H\left(0,\frac{1}{1-z}\,;1\right)}{2 z-2 \text{zb}}-\frac{4 H\left(0,\frac{1}{1-\text{zb}}\,;1\right)}{2 z-2 \text{zb}}-\frac{2 H\left(\frac{1}{1-z \text{zb}},\frac{1}{1-z}\,;1\right)}{2 z-2 \text{zb}}+\frac{2 H\left(\frac{1}{1-z \text{zb}},\frac{1}{1-\text{zb}}\,;1\right)}{2 z-2 \text{zb}}-\frac{\log ^2(z)}{2 z-2 \text{zb}}+\frac{\log ^2(\text{zb})}{2 z-2 \text{zb}}-\frac{2 \log (z) \log \left(\frac{(z-1) (\text{zb}-1)}{z \text{zb}}\right)}{2 z-2 \text{zb}}+\frac{2 \log (\text{zb}) \log \left(\frac{(z-1) (\text{zb}-1)}{z \text{zb}}\right)}{2 z-2 \text{zb}}\right)+O\left(\varepsilon^1\right)
